qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary file write via path traversal in CreateNewDAG API endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable function (CreateNewDAG in dags.go), same missing ValidateDAGName check, same generateFilePath path traversal in store.go line 493-498, same exploit path (POST /api/v1/dags with name containing path separator), and same root cause (user-controlled name passed to filepath.Abs without confinement to baseDir).

selected_finding.title: Arbitrary file write via path traversal in CreateNewDAG API endpoint

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The finding identifies the exact advisory-described vulnerability: CreateNewDAG in dags.go skips ValidateDAGName, allowing user-controlled names with path separators to reach generateFilePath in store.go, which resolves absolute paths via filepath.Abs without confining to baseDir, enabling arbitrary file write. The finding's code locations (dags.go:155, store.go:209-221, store.go:493-502) directly match the advisory's affected code (dags.go:120-170, store.go:493-498, store.go:213). The PoC reproduction was previously verified as successful (poc_report.json: verification_passed=true), but the judge PoC execution failed due to network unavailability preventing git clone of the target repository.
evidence: Finding code location internal/service/frontend/api/v1/dags.go:155 matches advisory dags.go:120-170 (CreateNewDAG handler missing ValidateDAGName call)
Finding code location internal/persis/filedag/store.go:493-502 matches advisory store.go:493-498 (generateFilePath resolves absolute path when name contains separator)
Finding code location internal/persis/filedag/store.go:209-221 matches advisory store.go:213 (Create writes to arbitrary path)
Finding exploit path POST /api/v1/dags -> CreateNewDAG -> dagStore.Create -> generateFilePath -> filepath.Abs matches advisory exploit narrative
Finding evidence at dags.go:273 confirms RenameDAG correctly validates with ValidateDAGName, matching advisory's description of the inconsistency
poc_report.json reports verification_passed=true with proof file created outside DAG directory containing EVOHUNT-PATH-TRAVERSAL-PROOF marker
blockers_to_higher_tier: Network connectivity to github.com is unavailable in the judge environment, preventing git clone of the target repository and PoC execution

poc_executions.observed_effect: PoC execution failed at the git clone step. The run.sh script attempts to clone https://github.com/dagu-org/dagu but network connectivity to github.com is unavailable in this environment (both host and Docker container). No vulnerable code was built or executed.
